title: Sindhu, Shetty Out of China Open After Wasting Match Points
indian_angle: India's top shuttlers PV Sindhu and Ayush Shetty faced early exits from the prestigious China Open Super 1000 tournament.
summary: PV Sindhu and Ayush Shetty both lost their respective matches at the China Open Super 1000, with Sindhu notably frittering away four match points against Chen Yufei.

title: Dhruv Negi Chuffed to Rediscover Form in Badminton
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Dhruv Negi expresses his delight in finding his rhythm and form on the badminton court.
summary: Young Indian shuttler Dhruv Negi is reportedly pleased with his return to form, signaling a positive development for his career.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System Implemented in Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has introduced a new 3x15 scoring system for domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in junior categories.
summary: BAI has announced the implementation of a 3x15 scoring system for U-13, U-15, and U-17 domestic ranking tournaments and national championships, aiming to debut it soon.

title: BAI Releases Eligibility for World Junior Championships 2026
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has released the eligibility criteria for Indian players aspiring to participate in the BWF World Junior Championships 2026 in Cairo.
summary: A circular from BAI outlines the player eligibility requirements for the upcoming BWF World Junior Championships 2026 in Cairo, Egypt, including selection tournament details.

title: Sindhu Claims Japan Open Crown, Answers Doubters with Emotional Win
indian_angle: PV Sindhu's triumphant return to form by winning the Japan Open Super 750 title is a major boost for Indian badminton.
summary: PV Sindhu ended her title drought by winning the Japan Open, defeating Akane Yamaguchi in a scorching final, a victory she described as emotional and a response to her critics.

title: Delhi World Championships Needs Gen Z, Alpha Watchers
indian_angle: A discussion on how the upcoming BWF World Championships in Delhi needs to attract younger Indian audiences, Gen Z and Gen Alpha, for its success.
summary: An analysis highlights the importance of engaging younger generations, Gen Z and Gen Alpha, to ensure a strong spectator base for the BWF World Championships 2026 to be held in Delhi.
